contents.value: **EVALUATE** — DAX keyword.
**DEFINE** — DAX keyword.
**MEASURE** — DAX keyword.
**VAR** — DAX keyword.
**RETURN** — DAX keyword.
**CALCULATE** — Built-in function/command in DAX.
**CALCULATETABLE** — Built-in function/command in DAX.
**FILTER** — Built-in function/command in DAX.
**ALL** — Built-in function/command in DAX.
**ALLEXCEPT** — Built-in function/command in DAX.

```sql
SELECT column1, column2 FROM table WHERE condition
```
**SELECT** retrieves data from one or more tables.

```sql
INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2)
```
**INSERT** adds new rows to a table.

```sql
UPDATE table SET col = val WHERE condition
```
**UPDATE** modifies existing rows.

```sql
DELETE FROM table WHERE condition
```
**DELETE** removes rows from a table.

```sql
SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id
```
**JOIN** combines rows from multiple tables based on a related column.

An **index** speeds up queries by creating an optimized lookup structure.
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx ON table(column);
```

A **view** is a virtual table defined by a query.
```sql
CREATE VIEW name AS SELECT ...;
```

A **transaction** groups operations that must succeed or fail as a unit.
```sql
BEGIN; ... COMMIT; -- or ROLLBACK;
```

**Aggregate functions** compute a single result from a set of rows.
```sql
COUNT(*), SUM(col), AVG(col), MIN(col), MAX(col)
```

A **subquery** is a query nested inside another query.
```sql
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM t2)
```

A **Common Table Expression** defines a named temporary result set.
```sql
WITH cte AS (SELECT ...) SELECT * FROM cte
```

**Constraints** enforce rules on data in tables.
```sql
PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, CHECK
```
**NULL** represents the absence of a value. Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL to test.

**Window functions** compute values across a set of rows related to the current row.
```sql
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col)
```

**GROUP BY** groups rows with the same values for aggregate calculations.
```sql
SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM emp GROUP BY dept
```

```msdax
RELATED(column) -> Scalar
```
Returns a related value from another table using an established relationship. Follows many-to-one relationships to retrieve a single value from the lookup table for each row context.

```msdax
RELATEDTABLE(table) -> Table
```
Returns a table of rows related to the current row context by following one-to-many relationships, equivalent to CALCULATETABLE with the related table applying row context as filter.

```msdax
SUMX(table, expression) -> Scalar
```
Iterates over each row of the specified table and evaluates the expression, then returns the sum of all evaluated values. Enables row-by-row calculations before aggregation.

```msdax
AVERAGEX(table, expression) -> Scalar
```
Iterates over each row of the specified table and evaluates the expression, then returns the arithmetic mean of all evaluated values. Blank values are excluded from the calculation.

```msdax
COUNTROWS(table) -> Integer
```
Counts the number of rows in the specified table or table expression, including rows with blank values. Returns a whole number representing the row count of the evaluated table.

```msdax
VALUES(column_or_table) -> Table
```
Returns a one-column table of distinct values from the specified column in the current filter context, including the blank row added for referential integrity violations.

```msdax
EARLIER(column, number) -> Scalar
```
Returns the value of the specified column in an outer row context when nested row contexts exist, commonly used in calculated columns to compare current row values against outer iterations.

```msdax
DIVIDE(numerator, denominator, alternate_result) -> Scalar
```
Performs safe division of numerator by denominator, returning the alternate result (default BLANK) when the denominator is zero, avoiding division-by-zero errors in measures.

```msdax
IF(logical_test, value_if_true, value_if_false) -> Scalar
```
Evaluates a logical condition and returns one of two values based on whether the condition is TRUE or FALSE, supporting conditional logic in calculated columns and measures.

```msdax
SWITCH(expression, value1, result1, ..., else_result) -> Scalar
```
Evaluates an expression against a list of values and returns the corresponding result, or an optional else result. More readable than nested IF statements for multi-condition logic.

```msdax
SELECTEDVALUE(column, alternate_result) -> Scalar
```
Returns the value when the filter context for the column has been filtered down to a single distinct value, otherwise returns the alternate result or BLANK for slicer-driven calculations.

```msdax
DATEADD(dates, number_of_intervals, interval) -> Table
```
Returns a table of dates shifted by the specified number of intervals (DAY, MONTH, QUARTER, YEAR) from the dates in the current filter context, enabling period-over-period time intelligence.

```msdax
TOTALYTD(expression, dates, filter, year_end_date) -> Scalar
```
Evaluates a year-to-date total for the given expression over the specified date column, optionally with a custom fiscal year-end date and additional filters for time intelligence.

```msdax
SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR(dates) -> Table
```
Returns a table containing a column of dates shifted one year back from the dates in the current filter context, commonly used within CALCULATE for year-over-year comparison measures.

```msdax
RANKX(table, expression, value, order, ties) -> Integer
```
Returns the ranking of a value in a table for the given expression, with configurable sort order (ASC or DESC) and tie-breaking behavior for SKIP or DENSE ranking.

```msdax
ISBLANK(value) -> Boolean
```
Returns TRUE if the specified value is BLANK, the special DAX empty state. Useful for handling missing data, null values, and referential integrity gaps in data models.
